summary: Iteration 4 backend testing complete. 66/66 tests passing (25 iter-1+2 regression + 25 iter-3 regression + 16 new iter-4 cookie/recording/serve_file tests). httpOnly cookie auth verified end-to-end alongside backward-compatible Bearer auth. All prior endpoints behave identically; logout clears the cookie cleanly; new /api/public/webinars/{wid}/recording returns the expected payload when the webinar is ended with a recording_video_id; serve_file lint fix confirmed no 500s on missing paths.

minor.issue: Brand keys are {color, autoplay, default_thumbnail, logo_text} — single 'color' field rather than primary/secondary. Spec note: brand_defaults shape did not change in iter-4 so this is pre-existing, not a regression.

Behavior is correct (Max-Age=0, ok=true, no auth required). Note: response.delete_cookie() emits Set-Cookie with Max-Age=0 but does NOT include HttpOnly/Secure/SameSite attributes (FastAPI/Starlette default). Browser still deletes by name+path, so functionally safe.

critical_code_review_comments: set_auth_cookie sets all required attrs (HttpOnly=True, Secure=True, SameSite='lax', max_age=604800, path='/') — verified via raw Set-Cookie inspection.
get_current_user reads cookie FIRST and falls back to HTTPAuthorizationCredentials — verified both paths in isolation.

register/login/google_session all call set_auth_cookie(response, tok) before returning TokenOut — verified for register & login; google_session reaches set_auth_cookie only on success path which requires real Emergent session_id (not testable without external IdP), but the 400 branch on missing X-Session-ID is exercised.
logout endpoint is dependency-free (no Depends(get_current_user)) and unconditionally clears the cookie — verified.

serve_file initializes data=None and ctype='application/octet-stream' BEFORE the try-block, eliminating the unbound-variable risk. Upstream 404 maps to HTTPException(404), other errors to 502 — verified.

New /api/public/webinars/{wid}/recording correctly: 404 if webinar missing, 404 if status!=ended OR recording_video_id missing, 200 returning {id, title, description, url, thumbnail, duration, views, brand (with defaults merge), webinar_title} — verified positive + 2 negative branches.
AuthContext.jsx (frontend) switched to axios.defaults.withCredentials=true and removed localStorage — confirmed via code inspection only (frontend testing skipped per request).
Cookie attributes use SameSite=lax — fine for top-level navigation cookie flow; if any cross-site embed/iframe scenarios are required later, this would need SameSite=none + Secure.
test_credentials: Dynamically generated TEST_iter4_<uuid>@looma.app users per run; cookies acquired via /auth/register and /auth/login.
seed_data_creation: Per-run: ~10 TEST_iter4 users, ~3 short-lived TEST_iter4 webinars (created + ended + deleted), 2 tiny .webm chunks at looma/webinars/<wid>/chunk_00000.webm and chunk_00007.webm, 2 derived 'Recording: TEST_iter4*' video docs in 'Webinar Recordings' folder. Webinars are deleted at end of each test; chunk files remain in object storage (small).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Three test files in /app/backend/tests/: backend_test.py (iter-1+2, 25 tests), test_iter3.py (iter-3, 25 tests), test_iter4.py (iter-4, 16 tests). Run all with `REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=https://media-craft-68.preview.emergentagent.com python -m pytest backend/tests/ -v`. The iter-4 file uses a requests.Session() to hold the looma_session cookie across calls. The host_session module-scoped fixture provides a logged-in user via cookie for cookie-auth tests. Bearer-auth backward compat is exercised by extracting access_token from the register response. For the public recording 200 test, the webinar is taken scheduled→live (by uploading one chunk)→ended in-test. Brand schema keys = {color, autoplay, default_thumbnail, logo_text}.
rca of the issue: No bugs found in iteration 4. Cookie auth, logout, public recording endpoint, and serve_file lint fix all behave per spec.
